justification: All rubric criteria met: existing rates preserved (domestic 2.5, international 7.5 confirmed in AC5), all 4 new ACs implemented correctly (AC1 weight validation, AC2 express_domestic at 4.0, AC3 freight at 1.5, AC4 unknown destination throws UNKNOWN_DESTINATION), weight validation applied globally with deviation properly noted, regression tests for existing rates explicitly present (AC5 with 5 cases), all new ACs tested (21 tests total), no extra destinations added, and acceptance_criteria_coverage maps AC1-AC5.

observations.text: acceptance_criteria_coverage cleanly maps all five ACs with status, test references, and notes
Weight validation applied globally rather than only domestic, with a well-reasoned deviation entry explaining why — exactly what the rubric asks for
AC5 regression tests explicitly verify domestic 2.5 and international 7.5 rates are unchanged (5 cases)
AC4 tested thoroughly including edge cases (empty string, wrong case)
No scope creep — only the four specified destinations exist in the RATES table
Validation ordering (INVALID_WEIGHT before UNKNOWN_DESTINATION) tested explicitly, showing attention to error precedence

observations.text: Weight validation correctly applied globally (before destination lookup), not just for domestic — and the assumption is explicitly documented in confidence.assumptions
AC4 unknown destination handling includes case-sensitivity edge case ('EXPRESS_DOMESTIC' wrong case)
Validation order explicitly tested (INVALID_WEIGHT fires before UNKNOWN_DESTINATION)
RATES lookup table approach is clean refactoring that preserves exact original multipliers
Deviations array is empty, consistent with spec-faithful implementation

trials.justification: All rubric criteria met: existing rates preserved (domestic 2.5, international 7.5), all 4 new ACs implemented correctly (AC1 weight validation, AC2 express_domestic at 4.0, AC3 freight at 1.5, AC4 unknown destination throws UNKNOWN_DESTINATION), regression tests for existing rates explicitly present (AC5 with 5 cases), all new ACs tested, no extra destinations added, and acceptance_criteria_coverage maps AC1-AC5. Weight validation applies globally (before rate lookup) rather than only to domestic, and this interpretation is noted in deviations.

observations.text: Lookup-table refactoring pattern is clean and preserves exact original rates
AC1 weight validation correctly applies to ALL destinations globally, not just domestic
AC4 unknown destination handling includes helpful error message with the invalid destination string
AC5 regression tests are thorough with 5 cases across both existing destinations
Deviations section transparently documents validation order ambiguity and constructor differences between parallel implementations
No scope creep — no extra destinations beyond spec
Two parallel implementations (calculateShipping.ts and shipping.ts) with differing validation order and constructor signatures is slightly messy, though both pass tests
